name: Chicken Piccata with Sweetcorn, Chilli and Buttermilk
description: Pounded chicken thighs dredged in flour and pan-fried until deeply golden, finished in a brisk buttermilk sauce with sweetcorn, anchovy, shallots, garlic and chilli. The buttermilk splits into small curds and emulsifies with butter into a rich, tangy sauce.
keywords: Chicken Piccata, Buttermilk Chicken, Sweetcorn Chicken, Chicken Thighs, Anchovy Butter Sauce, Piccata
recipeIngredient: 1 mild red chilli, thinly sliced (or ¾ tsp red pepper flakes)

recipeInstructions.name: Prep
recipeInstructions.text: Thinly slice the shallots, garlic and chilli. Shuck the corn and remove the kernels.

recipeInstructions.name: Pound the Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: Place a chicken thigh between two sheets of greaseproof paper and pound with a rolling pin or heavy skillet to roughly 1cm thick all over. Season with salt and pepper. Repeat with the remaining thighs.

recipeInstructions.name: Dredge
recipeInstructions.text: Put the flour in a shallow bowl. Thoroughly dredge each chicken thigh in flour, shaking off any excess. Arrange on a large lipped tray.

recipeInstructions.name: Pan-Fry the Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: Heat a large cast-iron skillet over high heat. Add about 2 tablespoons of olive oil and heat until wisps of smoke appear. Gently lay 2 thighs in the skillet and cook for 4–5 minutes until deeply golden underneath. Flip and cook for 2 minutes more. Transfer to a plate browned side up. Repeat with remaining thighs, adding more oil as needed.

recipeInstructions.name: Build the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Turn the heat to low. Add 2 tablespoons of butter, the anchovy fillets, shallots, garlic, chilli and 6 mint sprigs. Season and sauté, stirring, for about 3 minutes until the aromatics are softened. Add the corn and cook for 2 minutes until just softened.

recipeInstructions.name: Add Buttermilk
recipeInstructions.text: Add the buttermilk and adjust the heat to a brisk simmer. Cook, shaking the pan and stirring occasionally, for 3–4 minutes until slightly reduced but still saucy. The buttermilk will split and you'll see small curds — this is completely normal and good.

recipeInstructions.name: Emulsify with Butter
recipeInstructions.text: Cube the remaining butter and add it to the pan. Cook for about 2 minutes, vigorously shaking the pan as you stir, until the butter is fully incorporated and a thick emulsion forms.

recipeInstructions.name: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Cut the lemons in half. Divide the chicken between plates, spoon over plenty of the sauce and top with fresh mint. Serve with lemon halves for squeezing.

notes: Don't be alarmed when the buttermilk splits — the curds are intentional and give the sauce its character.
Vigorous shaking of the pan while adding the butter is key to building the emulsion — it won't come together if you're too gentle.
Pounding the thighs to an even thickness ensures they cook evenly and quickly without drying out.
Fresh mint is the right call here over basil for brightness, but basil works if that's what you have.
